danger_signals.visual_form: On-screen wind direction and strength display
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental hazard affecting ball trajectory; player must compensate or face poor shot outcome

danger_signals.visual_form: Ball position indicator showing rough, sand, or water proximity
danger_signals.meaning: Ball is in hazardous position; next shot will be constrained or penalized

danger_signals.visual_form: Hole layout complexity shown in bird's-eye view; terrain obstacles visible
danger_signals.meaning: Course presents increasing challenge; requires skill to complete with minimal strokes

safety_signals.visual_form: Ball resting on clear green fairway in bird's-eye view
safety_signals.meaning: Optimal positioning; next shot will perform as intended without hazard interference

safety_signals.visual_form: Princess Toadstool (Peach) or Princess Daisy present on screen providing tips
safety_signals.meaning: Information and support available; player can make informed decisions about club selection and shot power

safety_signals.visual_form: Clear, visible power meter during swing phase
safety_signals.meaning: Shot mechanics are controlled and transparent; player has agency over outcome

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Mario character sprite with signature cap
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Associated with Luigi as opponent in multiplayer; caddie is Princess Toadstool (Peach)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Luigi character sprite with green hat
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Multiplayer opponent role; caddie is Princess Daisy

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: In Japanese version (Mario Open Golf), only Japan Course is visible at start; other courses unlock after completion
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must complete courses sequentially; progression gated by successful course completion

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: International release features simplified hole layouts compared to Japanese release; hazards and obstacles reduced
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player faces reduced challenge in international version; same course designs reused but with modified difficulty parameters

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Wind direction and strength displayed as on-screen indicator affecting ball trajectory
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Ball physics change based on wind; player must adapt swing power and direction accordingly

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Different courses represent international locations (Japan, USA, Australia); background and terrain assets change per course
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Hole layouts vary by location; course-specific hazard patterns and challenge types

environmental_storytelling.example: Australia, Japan, and USA courses each have distinct hole layouts reflecting actual international golf courses; visual backdrop and terrain communicate location without explicit text
Princess Toadstool serves as Mario's caddie; Princess Daisy serves Luigi's caddie in multiplayer. Their presence in the UI communicates character relationships and support dynamic without dialogue
Bird's-eye view for course planning + third-person perspective for shot execution communicates progression from strategic thinking to tactical action
Water, sand traps, and rough terrain are visible in overhead view; ball lie position shows whether player is in safe or compromised situation

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Positioned in third-person perspective; power meter active; waiting for player input to determine shot strength
Takes shots sequentially with Mario; competes for lowest stroke count per hole
Trajectory affected by selected club, power input, spin, and wind conditions; all factors visualized to show cause-and-effect
Provides tips and information on screen; guides player toward optimal club and shot strategy

notes: This is a golf sports game, not a narrative RPG or faction-based world-state simulator. Danger and safety signals relate to game mechanics (hazards, positioning, wind) rather than world narrative. Faction indicators refer to playable characters rather than political factions. The game communicates world-state primarily through course design progression, difficulty scaling between regional versions, and environmental golf hazards. No overt political or faction conflict system exists in the source material.
